fields.title: Brian Kohout on Trig's
fields.author: Brian Kohout
fields.body:
Trig’s Grocery and Liquor has always been a fantastic place to shop, but what truly sets it apart is the exceptional customer service, especially from long-time employee Julie. Her dedication and warm personality make every visit special, and her commitment to providing top-notch service is unmatched.

Every year during the Pond Hockey Tournament, we look forward to seeing her familiar, friendly face. Julie goes above and beyond to make customers feel welcome, and her years of service have made a lasting impact on your community. It’s rare to find someone who brings so much passion and care to their job, and we truly appreciate everything she does.

Thank you, Julie, for your years of outstanding service—we can’t wait to see you again next year! And thank you, Trig’s, for fostering such a wonderful shopping experience.

fields.title: Doreen K on Burnt Bridge Tavern
fields.author: Doreen K
fields.body:
This property is really interesting. It is a stop for many UTV drivers.
There are two buildings one is an outdoor bar. That is a small building up on a hill, which has amazing views. The tavern itself is small and cozy inside with really cool log/wood furniture/tables and chairs.
Overall, a five star for atmosphere. Here is the part that isn’t so good and which is very disappointing.
They seem to be understaffed, but this is the second time I’ve been here so really there’s no excuses. There was two women in front of me and my boyfriend. It took over 15 minutes for anyone to even acknowledge that we wanted to sit down for a meals. The woman kept walking by completely, ignoring the fact that we were all standing there. In fact, there’s really nowhere to stand you kind of stand in between the bar and the dining tables. Finally, the bartender told us we can sit at the table that was right by us. She brought us some menus got us some drinks and we ordered quickly because we figured if we didn’t we wouldn’t eat at all anytime soon. My boyfriend being a diabetic needed to eat. We ordered chili; good, hardy, but it lacked flavor.  We also ordered a pizza that again lacked flavor, but it had an ok amount of toppings, but they were not evenly spread.  I had to put a lot of Parmesan just to get even somewhat of good flavor. My boyfriend‘s sandwich was also mediocre. Very disappointing since we drove over 45 minutes to get there. You know what we might try it again because I believe in giving a place three tries.
Seriously, though as an owner, you should have your staff greet people whether you’re busy or not. We could see if she was busy but a simple hey take a seat wherever you like or I’ll be right with you. Goes a long way.

fields.author: Ted
fields.body: Love Craftsman American Tavern in Eagle River WI.  Today was a flight of beer from Blueberry Train Tribute Brewing in Eagle River,  Hughes crawler Stout from Fox River Oshkosh & a Campfire Old Fashioned with Smoked Maple Knob Creek Bourbon with toasted marshmallow and cinnamon.  Fish tacos and Mint Smash burger with tots were both excellent as well as our attentive server Susan.  Fun & delicious visit

fields.title: Jorja Larsen on The Craftsman American Tavern
fields.author: Jorja Larsen
fields.body: Such a great spot for supper! We popped in on a later Friday afternoon. Everything was amazing. They did say they were understaffed and getting a table might take a minute, we didn’t mind, we were just happy to be there! All of the design and decor added to a welcoming and rustic atmosphere. Once we got seated, it didn’t take more than 5 minutes, we had drinks right away. I had the love, Stella. It was super smooth. The cheese curds with the raspberry sauce was amazing. For supper we both had burgers! Everything was great! Our server was Brittney and she was so kind! I would 100% recommend anybody to go here and grab a bite to eat!

fields.title: Nibs on The Craftsman American Tavern
fields.author: Nibs
fields.body: Outstanding lunch. Modern-vibe lunch spot in the heart of Eagle River. Stopped in here based on the good reviews and we weren't disappointed. From the top notch cocktails to the fresh ingredients in the food, this place knocked it out of the park. Plump, juicy burgers and loaded sandwiches, not to mention the largest portion of cheese curds I've ever seen as an appetizer! Excellent service, friendly and attentive but not overbearing. Even a proper shuffleboard available for a bit of fun. Would definitely recommend eating here if you're in town.

fields.author: Deborah Rayala
fields.body: OMG!!  What an excellent experience!!  My husband, daughter, and I stayed over Memorial Day and met a group of friends . . .  the cabin was "rustic" and spotless!!  The owners went out of their way to make sure everything, ABSOLUTELY EVERYTHING, was perfect!!  The fishing on the lake was beyond spectacular!!  We couldn't get our rods baited fast enough!!  The blue gill fish fry we had was spectacular!  Great cleaning facility!!  Campgrounds were clean and finished.  Felt very safe sending the kids on their own.  The horses were a bonus!!  The kids loved them (so did the adults)!!!!  Will definitely be returning!!!!  Can't wait!!!!

fields.title: Tracey Rohde on Krull's Deer Path Resort
fields.author: Tracey Rohde
fields.body:
My family and I rented Cubs Den for over two months this past winter. We left our gear, sleds and provisions in the cabin. So nice not have to worry about loading and unloading after a three hour drive. We loved being right on the snowmobile trail! Jay and Sue are the perfect hosts. So kind, and caring. It snows a lot, as you can image, and they both worked so hard to ensure we had clean walk ways. Above and beyond! Cabins are clean, well appointed, and cozy. We have already booked Birch View for next riding season. The other folks that stayed there, top notch - we all had dinner together toward the end of the season. So many great memories.
FYI ~ Our friends, also with two kids rented Otter's Lair. They have nothing but great things to say as well.
We made great friends for many years to come!

fields.author: Adam Robards
fields.body:
My family and I had the pleasure of staying at Deer Path for the first time and for me to say we will return is an understatement!

Cabins and grounds are very clean and well maintained.  Owners are very friendly and will make you feel right at home during your stay with them!  The beach is an awesome amenity for kids to play with plenty of lounging for adults near by to keep an eye on the little ones.  Lake is not over crowded which we loved as well and the cabins had everything we could have possibly needed and then some to make a very seamless stay.

Would highly recommend this resort to anyone looking for a very relaxed and family friendly environment with fantastic owners who will treat you like their own!  We will without a doubt be returning 😍

fields.title: Mary Lynn Kilberry on Andy's Pontoon Saloon
fields.author: Mary Lynn Kilberry
fields.body: Us Illinoisans have been coming up to Eagle River since our childhood days as it holds a special place in our hearts. Andy's Pontoon Saloon has made our visit's more memorable and enjoyable! The atmosphere is dynamic with great bands playing Wednesday - Sunday afternoons. We love their pizza! They serve Wisconsin craft beer's and I tried their Lite Pontoon Saloon beer and it tasted delicious! They also serve wines, seltzers, NA beer & soda's. They have  humongous pretzels also. The outdoor beer garden is family & dog friendly and they have a pontoon boat you can chill out in or take pictures. You can play a game of bags and they also have trivia, bingo & yoga night. In the cooler months there is a fire pontoon outside you can sit by enjoying your drinks. fields.title: Jamie Chapman on Bauer's Dam Resort
fields.author: Jamie Chapman
fields.body:
This is a review for the restaurant.

We (my family and I) come to Bauer's Dam restaurant every summer when we come up to our cabin. It has always been a pleasant experience and we never had any problems.

Sadly, this is the last time we will be coming to Bauer's Dam.

I called the restaurant to order food to pick up. The person who answered the phone said that I could just come to the restaurant and order the food to take home when I got there. I was annoyed because we had a big order and I knew it would take a little bit to make. I got to the restaurant and not a single place to sit, not even at the bar. I put the order in and they said it would be an hour.

This was the most annoying part, there's nowhere to sit down and wait for my order. My mom found a chair at the bar, one single chair and of course she ordered a drink. Which was the restaurant's intention this whole time. When someone is trying to stop drinking and you bring them to a bar to wait an hour for their food chances are they will give in to having a drink. This is why I ordered pick up.

I ordered a burger which comes with a side of french fries. I wanted french fries and they were out of them. I wasn't given an option for the other sides that I can choose from. I was charged for it regardless. I asked for the burger to be well done and the burger was far from well done.

When someone calls for food to be picked up, take their order like EVERY OTHER RESTAURANT and do not haggle them to come in now to order and wait for it there to take home. The point of calling prior is to get the order in so it is almost done or ready when you arrive. When you order food to pick up that usually is because you don't want to go inside only to pick up the food.

Please don't waste your time coming here.

fields.title: Lake Edwards on Jensen-Akins Hardware & Appliance
fields.author: Lake Edwards
fields.body:
When shopping for appliances for my new home, I made a point to support a local business. My family has worked with Jensen’s in the past with minimal issues, so I felt comfortable starting there. I knew exactly what I wanted and requested a quote, but I was told they could not provide their “best price” until I placed an order. I took the same specifications to another retailer, who was able to give me their best price immediately.

I returned to Jensen’s to give them another opportunity, but once again was told pricing would only be finalized after placing the order. Ultimately, I decided to move forward with Jensen’s anyway, even though their price ended up being $839 higher than the competitor.

The installation experience is where the biggest issues arose. I was given an afternoon delivery window, but the installers were delayed at another job and did not arrive until after 5:00 PM. It was pouring rain at the time, and instead of backing up to my garage to unload, they unloaded in my driveway. As a result, the appliances were brought into my home soaked. Because my driveway is gravel, a rock became lodged in one of the refrigerator wheels, which caused significant scratching to my brand-new flooring.

I notified the store as soon as I was able to document the damage with photos. Their solution was to send their own “fixer” to replace the damaged floorboards. That individual called me after 8:00 PM to explain his plan, which felt unprofessional. I informed my sales representative that I preferred to have my original flooring contractor handle the repair instead.

Additionally, because propane had not yet been connected to the house, the delivery team assured me it would be no issue for them to return later to hook up the range. However, Jensen’s later charged me an additional fee for this visit, which took approximately five minutes. Given the prior issues, I would have expected this to be handled as a courtesy.

The final frustration came when my contractor went to install the refrigerator water line and discovered that no hose had been provided. One was not included or even offered at the time of purchase, requiring me to make an extra trip to buy it myself. For a full kitchen and laundry appliance package, this was an unexpected oversight.

Overall, I was very disappointed with my experience at Jensen Aiken’s.

fields.body: Such a refreshing place to buy my appliances. I have been buying my appliances here for the past 30 years or so. In a world where you might have to search to find customer service or even a human to talk to when buying something (box stores, warehouse stores), it is refreshing to see real people that you have known for decades that are there for you when you need a new appliance. And the prices really are better than the big stores too. My Whirlpool washing machine that I had bought at Jensen Akins from Debbie 20 years ago finally wore out....guess what?...I walked in on a Tuesday at 9 am and there she was!  They had just what I needed on the floor and I was able to have it loaded up within an hour. The whole team is great! It is always great to see Debbie there too! I'll definitely be back! Thank you!!

fields.body: We purchased our washer and dryer from them and the service,  scheduling, and professionalism was fantastic.  I called to have someone help with a stove conversion from gas to LP and the technician arrived on schedule but was extremely unprofessional.  I asked him to take his shoes off and he said he couldn't due to insurance reasons (totally reasonable) he acted a little upset to go back to the truck for shoe covers. He began to start working on the stove and I hear him yell "Jesus Christ" loudly.  He immediately started throwing a fit about the parts that were taken off before he got there (which I told him about before hand on the phone). He didn't want to touch anything after that. He repeated over and over how he's not going to go any further with it. I really didn't have a problem with him explaining his concerns (they were not unreasonable), but he was extremely disrespectful and unprofessional.  My wife and I couldn't believe how he acted. I called a local handyman and he was respectful, professional and took care of it with no problem.

fields.title: Laura Simms on Chanticleer Inn
fields.author: Laura Simms
fields.body: I was there for an ice driving school. I had room 42 in the A frame bldg. It faced the lake. The bed was comfortable. There was a small counter in the kitchen. I put my laptop on that and got some work done the day I arrived (they let me check in early). The food was great and the staff was terrific. The only noise was from snowmobiles. It looks like it would be a nice spot for a summer family vacation if I still had little kids.

fields.title: Courtney Johnson on Chanticleer Inn
fields.author: Courtney Johnson
fields.body:
We visited after seeing on the website that they offered a breakfast buffet. The restaurant itself looked nice and had a great location by the lake, which was a plus. However, the breakfast was disappointing and definitely not worth the $15 charge.
The buffet offered very basic items — the kind of food you'd typically get for free at most mid-range hotels. The eggs and French toast sticks tasted like they came straight from a box, and the strawberries were unfortunately moldy, which was really off-putting.
Overall, we expected a higher quality breakfast for the price, especially given the setting. It felt like a missed opportunity to deliver something memorable.

fields.title: David Engman on Chanticleer Inn
fields.author: David Engman
fields.body:
Went for Friday fish fry by boat, and had an excellent experience! The food was delicious, especially the clam chowder, the fried perch, and the steak. Also, the brandy old fashioned was perfectly mixed. We heard that the kitchen was down a person due to an illness, but it didn't show at all as everything came out on time and was perfectly cooked.
Prices were very reasonable for a nice restaurant on the water.
I highly recommend this place!

fields.title: Mike Hameister on Chanticleer Inn
fields.author: Mike Hameister
fields.body:
Great experience!
Great staff!
Great food!
Great view!
Great date with my wife!

The Perch was lightly breaded and fried just right. Steak prepared as ordered.

This place is tucked away, access by boat, road, or trail. Very charming indoor, outdoor, and bar seating areas. They have room for you, don't miss out.
We don't have a need to stay there, but I certainly can't see why it would be any different than our dining experience. Try it for yourself, or send over flow guests there to experience the Northwoods.

fields.title: Mario on Toad in the Hole | Breakfast • Lunch
fields.author: Mario
fields.body:
I’m usually NOT a breakfast guy, but Toad in the Hole is an absolute game changer. If you’re not a breakfast person, you NEED to come here — they will change your mind, no question.

The eggs were insanely fresh, like farm-to-plate fresh. The scrambled eggs were cooked perfectly—soft, fluffy, seasoned just right, never dry. I swear for a second I thought Gordon Ramsay was back there making them for me.

What really sets this place apart is that everything is made right in front of you. No mystery, no “what’s going on in the back” feeling—just clean, open cooking where you can see the quality and care firsthand.

Everything is made fresh daily—homemade sausage links and sausage patties using their own recipe, fresh farm eggs, and you can taste the difference in every bite. The restaurant is super clean, the atmosphere is on point, and the staff is awesome—friendly, professional, and locked in.

The French toast sticks are the best I’ve ever had, hands down. And the sausage patties? Unreal. Best I’ve ever eaten.

This place is a straight 10 out of 10. Absolute must-try. I’ll definitely be back.

fields.title: Eric Boesen on Toad in the Hole | Breakfast • Lunch
fields.author: Eric Boesen
fields.body:
If you blink, you might miss it—but trust me, don’t. This tiny little spot in Eagle River is absolute breakfast/brunch gold.

First off, this place is SMALL. Like “you’ll probably wait in line and question your life choices” small. But once you get in, everything changes. The food? Unreal. We’re talking scratch-made, creative, and somehow both fancy and comfort food at the same time. You can tell they actually care about what they’re putting on your plate.

The vibe is cozy, quirky, and feels like you stumbled into a hidden Northwoods gem that locals secretly don’t want you to find out about. Service is friendly, fast, and makes you feel like a regular even on your first visit.

Pro tip: don’t come here if you’re in a rush… come here if you want one of the best breakfasts you’ve had in a long time.

Bottom line: small place, big flavors, totally worth the wait. I’d fight a line of hungry tourists to eat here again.

fields.title: Seth Boodle on Toad in the Hole | Breakfast • Lunch
fields.author: Seth Boodle
fields.body:
**A Hidden Gem in Eagle River – Worth the Wait!**

This restaurant is an absolute treasure, tucked away in the most unexpected corner of Eagle River. From the moment we stepped in, the atmosphere was warm and inviting, perfectly complementing the incredible food. Watching the chef skillfully craft each dish was a highlight—it felt like witnessing culinary magic!

Be sure to arrive early or prepare for a wait (it’s a small space with only 20 seats), but trust me, every minute is worth it. The intimate setting only enhances the experience, making it feel special and unique. We loved every bite, every moment—this place is truly one of a kind. #Ubepancakes…and more

fields.body: Great place to stay! Rooms 118 and 120 are on the opposite side of the pool, they are on the backside of the Inn, in between the lobby and the north end of the Inn. fields.author: Who Dis
fields.body:
First off, this place has one of the best bougie bathrooms I've ever been in. It's the kind of bathroom you'd feel guilty taking a poo in.

With that said the place is awesome. I got the campfire coffee and it was excellent. Good body and not to sweet like a lot of coffee drinks. The outdoor patio is quaint and plenty of extra seating on the lawn with picnic tables.

Just a nice place to chill out at in the country just before a hike. Or after.

We've had breakfast here twice. Both times my husband ordered a breakfast burrito. Both times they forgot about it, and he had to go ask for it.

This is a very cute place. Their coffee and pastries are good, but 2 out of 2 with forgotten orders is ridiculous. The first time, they weren't busy at all, with one or two other customers. The second time, they were pretty slammed, but since it had already happened the only other time we'd been here, I'm really not prepared to give them the benefit of the doubt.

fields.title: Ron T on Kickback Grill
fields.author: Ron T
fields.body:
My wife and I travel a lot. I’ve begun to dread eating out. Overpriced mediocre food. Pretentious descriptions of dishes that turn out to be disappointing. Rude personnel.
So when we stopped here for lunch I wasn’t expecting much.
I’m happy to say none of that applies here. Our lunch was delicious. The food was unique and delicious. My wife ordered the Naked Poppers for an appetizer. I usually don’t like poppers which seem to be exactly alike everywhere. Not here. These jalapeños were tender, wrapped in bacon and filled with creamy, melted cream cheese. Awesome.
I had the special, gourmet grilled cheese. It was very filling and heavy with delicious vegetables. I had the haystack onion rings and they were finely drizzled with parmesan cheese. Delicious.
My wife had a burger. I was too busy eating mine to see what kind she had.  Every staff person was happy and gracious.

fields.title: Christin Shouse on Quiet Comfort Cabins
fields.author: Christin Shouse
fields.body:
I’m torn between three and four stars.
After canceling our summer vacation out of state due to Covid, we booked here. I searched every picture I could find worried about cabin location, how close we’d be to neighbors, ect
As soon as we pulled up I was unhappy. We were about 8 feet from a large group of people staying in between cabins, in an older house. Not seen from any pictures I had searched.
We had a boat, they had a boat and they were spread out everywhere, even though we shared a driveway, they kept their stuff all over. On several occasions we had to tell them we wanted to leave so they’d pick up their stuff.
Finally getting into the cabins. They were no frills and pretty much everything we expected. The window AC didn’t really reach all the bedrooms so they were hot. The toilet seat was extremely low, not a big deal for most but for me, with double hip replacements it was miserable.
Being right on the lake was great. Loved the dock and the view... until those cabin neighbors blew up two huge inflatables. So our beautiful lake view was now a view of a chicken and a flamingo. So frustrating especially because no one used them. One cabin neighbor was actually so annoyed he went into the water and move them. That was a mood killer for sure. This is why I wanted to be far away from neighbors and these cabins are much closer than we anticipated, another cabin visitor shared our surprise. Overall the trip was okay but again, being so close really ruined our trip. I mean during our evening fire pit, we got to listen to those awesome neighbors urinating in the water. Certainly not the vacation we had in mind.
One thing that really bothered us- they ask you to strip the sheets upon check out, and leave the sheets on the kitchen table. That is disgusting and we did not participate in that. That’s a policy that needs changed ASAP.
I will say the owners were very nice and a pleasure to deal with.

fields.title: Aidan E on Hillbilly Hollow
fields.author: Aidan E
fields.body:
I am not a cheese enthusiast. But I do have one complaint for this particular fromagerie, and not because of its products (although I have heard some poor reviews).

The name of this cheesery has caused me a great deal of pain and misunderstanding. Never before have I been so devastated to learn the name of a homonym as I had been when I discovered that this cheesery was called Hillbilly Hollow too. For there is another Hillbilly Hollow, one located in Central Wisconsin, that is revered by the locals as a climbing epicenter. When I was looking forward to going climbing for a weekend, I was researching nearby crags to my current residence, Land O Lakes, WI. One of the first (and only, Northern Wisconsin has very few climbing places) that showed up was none other than "Hillbilly Hollow." I understandably mistook this Hillbilly Hollow to be the place I could climb, and when I found out, it was too late.

I decided, before realizing my mistake, to head out to this Hillbilly Hollow for weekend to climb. I logged the coordinates into my cracked iPhone 4s, threw on some camo sweatpants and my Three Wolf Moon shirt (which I highly recommend, if only for its magical attributes), and headed out. It was only a fifteen minute drive from Land-O on Google Maps, but I pushed my 9370R John Deere with 370 horsepower engine and managed to make it in under 30. When I arrived, I saw no crag. There was no wall. No hulking mass of stratum. Instead, I saw only a cheese stand. In fact, it was not just a cheese stand; it was a goat's cheese stand. The man at the stand took no notice of me as I was driving up, but when I got out of the tractor, he immediately began to give me a great deal of respect. This I can only attribute to the Three Wolf Moon shirt. He politely told me that this was not, in fact, a crag. Rather, it was a shack on the side of the road. Pity. I later discovered that the crag was in Central Wisconsin, but as I said, by then it proved to be too late.

I would also like to take this space to commend the Three Wolf Moon shirt for the amount it helps in making one seem credible. Thank you Three Wolf Moon shirt.

fields.title: C.J. Robinson on Bur’s Black Bear Bungalow
fields.author: C.J. Robinson
fields.body:
We stayed in Nic and Marlo's Black Bear Bungalow condo over the extended 4th of July holiday weekend and really enjoyed our stay. The condo is part of the former Black Bear Lodge, located on Little Saint Germain Lake's most desirable peninsula.
The condo was freshly painted, very clean and well appointed with beautiful new major and small kitchen appliances, washer and dryer. Though we brought all of our own dishes, utensils, towels, etc. we found the kitchen cabinets were well stocked with dishes, glassware, kitchen utensils, towels and consumable supplies.
We were pleased with the large master bedroom (King Bed) + master bathroom and second bedroom (2x Twin Beds + Pack-n-Play crib) with a second bathroom down the hall. Multiple closets in the bedrooms, bathroom and hallway offered generous storage to accommodate our family of family of 5.
A beautiful new gas fireplace insert was installed in the open concept living room area days prior to our visit and a comfortable, large couch appeared fresh out of the showroom.
High speed internet, WiFi, board games and large, flat panel Smart TV's in the living room and master bedroom kept us connected and entertained when it rained.
We used the permanently installed charcoal grills, picnic tables and playground equipment just outside of the unit daily and noticed a dedicated boat slip was available ~50-feet from the sliding back patio door.
Thanks to Nic and Marlo for a great stay... We'll be back again later this month!

fields.title: Mark Beemsterboer on Bay View, Vacation Rental House
fields.author: Mark Beemsterboer
fields.body:
This is my favorite cabin at the Estrold Resort. I usually visit when the weather is a bit chilly, and it’s great to come back to the cabin after a day of hiking; the fireplace is always a treat. The three bedrooms and two bathrooms are spacious, though one has to walk through the middle bedroom to get to the back bedroom. Cabins are not situated on top of each other at this resort. The dining table is huge, perfect for meals or evening board games. There are also a couple of bar seats at the kitchen’s window counter. Picture windows in the dining room look out at the resort, and the picture windows in the living room look out at the lake.

This cabin sits on a slight rise, overlooking Little St. Germain Lake. The walk down to the private pier is easy with a couple of flights of stairs with railings. There’s a fire pit behind the cabin, also looking over the lake. Plenty of parking in the private driveway.

I was allowed to walk through almost every cabin at the Estrold Resort in the past—they’re all fantastic—but the Bayview is simply special to me. It’s a comfortable, handsome cabin.

There are so many little nice touches that the owner does to make your stay nicer. For example - laundry detergent for the washer and dryer. So conveniently located near bars, restaurants, donut shop, coffee shop, grocery store...everything you would need. Also very close to mini golf and other activities for the kids. We did not want to leave and cannot wait to come back!

fields.title: Jessica Golos on Circle of Pines
fields.author: Jessica Golos
fields.body:
Had an okay experience here. The lower pine cabin had a nice view, but not the cleanest. The shower liner looked as if it hadn’t been changed in a very long time. The heater didn’t work, so they provided a rolling space heater. It felt like you were walking on a slant throughout the entire cabin. There is a very soft spot between the kitchen and living room where it felt like you would fall thru the floor.
We rented a pontoon for a day, but we were told from the owner that if “another person wanted to rent the pontoon for more days, then he would give it to them instead because of more money. “That really didn’t sit right with us. Kind of arrogant of him, and we actually left a day early.

fields.title: Eric Boesen on Riverstone Brewing Co.
fields.author: Eric Boesen
fields.body:
Riverstone Brewing Company is an awesome spot right in the heart of Eagle River. The atmosphere hits that perfect Northwoods vibe—laid-back, welcoming, and a great place to hang out whether you’re with friends or family.

The beer selection is impressive with plenty of house brews on tap, plus something for everyone even if craft beer isn’t your thing. The bourbon selection is a nice bonus too. Food was just as good—classic bar favorites done right (definitely recommend the cheese curds and burgers).

Service was friendly and kept things moving, and the whole place just has a fun, lively energy—especially if you’re catching a game or live music.

Overall, it’s one of those places you’ll want to come back to anytime you’re up north. Great drinks, good food, and an easygoing vibe. 🍻

fields.author: Ari Rosengarten

Hiller Vacation Homes has been a fantastic partner for me as a host in the North Woods. For anyone looking for help with managing their property in the North Woods area, I would recommend reaching out to Hiller Vacation Homes. They're extremely responsive, helpful, and knowledgeable partners who are capable of helping to ensure their managed properties are treated as their own homes.

With their experience in the area and their insights of the industry, they help to remove much of the anxiety of owning a home away from home.

When I contacted Hiller Homes I was basically told there’s nothing they can do. I am confident these snakes are poisonous/lethal and this is an unsafe environment for families to stay in. Will never stay at this location or rent from Hiller Homes ever again.

fields.body: We stayed at the Deerpath on Elbert’s Resort. This cabin is great for large families. It is clean and well kept and decorated beautifully. 5 bedroom plus a partially enclosed loft. Plenty of living space for the adults and kids. This resort is amazing! Restaurant and bar is on site, volleyball and basketball and park for the children next to an open field for play. Private beach access to renters of the resort and each cabin gets a boat slip on Little St. Germain lake with their rental. If you’re up in winter  it’s beautiful as well.

fields.title: Stephanie Wells on Knocker's Pizza Company Inc.
fields.author: Stephanie Wells
fields.body: Took my elderly mother here for a Friday fish fry. Clean pleasant restaurant.  The hostess and every waitress seemed very young. Our waitress seemed to be in a bad mood. Our food took a long time -about 40 minutes to get our food and it wasn't very busy as far as tables filled. But we are ok waiting. We know it's tourist season. After food was delivered, I wasn't served, but figured the tray was full and she'd be back. Maybe 6 minutes goes by, me wondering where mine is, waitress finally approaches me, said there was a mistake and the bottom of our ticket didn't print so my food wasn't made. She didn't say I'm sorry, or express any concern. Just like, too bad then walked away. So I sat. The rest of my family ate. Maybe 12 minutes later, I got my food. Waitress plops it down without a word and walks away. Not a so sorry. Nothing. I'm an understanding person and I can take it when mistakes are made, but I have to say that girl's attitude sorta ruined the whole thing for me. It's expensive to go out to eat, and part of eating out is the experience.  My experience was watching everyone else eat while our waitress skulked around looking crabby and couldn't even express some empathy that I was made to wait. I will say, the 3 bites I got of my food before we packed up (because the teens were antsy) was good. Teen liked pizza, son like tenderloin salad. Maybe i'd get takeout from here but probably would avoid dining in. Of course no discount for having to watch everyone eat then leave hungry with my food in a box. They charged full price. The teens in the floor need to learn some customer service skills. Hopefully you'll get better service than we did but you will like the food most likely.

fields.body:
I always get a good meal at knockers.  My favorites are the Friday night fish fry and the New Yorker sandwich.  On this visit, they had a lasagna special and I decided to give it a try. I'm very glad I did because it was fantastic. The lasagna was filled with meat and cook perfectly. It came with sauteed vegetables and breadsticks. I can't say enough good things about this special.

Update: Friday night, I had the special which was fried bluegill and it was done perfectly.  I normally have the perch which is also very good.

Got the info off the lantern I needed and went to the bait shop / sporting good store pretty much a block down fry in there. They had what I needed for half the price of the Hank's hardware price and were very pleasant to deal with. I will also be reviewing them and giving them a good review.

Won't be back to this place, they seem to think they're the only game in town and don't care about the customer.

fields.title: Mark Beemsterboer on Idle Hours Resort
fields.author: Mark Beemsterboer
fields.body:
I've stayed at Idle Hours three times, now, and I've been nothing but impressed with the resort. Idle Hours is located on Little Saint about as far from Highway 70 as it gets, which makes for a quieter stay on the lake.

This resort is family-owned, which is rarer today, as many resorts are condo associations. Sally and Tom are terrific people.

The grounds and cabins are always clean and in great condition. I visit St. Germain in October and April every year, so I appreciate the off-season pricing.

Most cabins have washers, dryers, and dishwashing machines. Recently, they added separate wifi for each cabin; another cabin's kids can't big down your connection. It's a welcome addition to the resort.

Book a cabin at Idle Hours. You won't regret it!

fields.title: Jennifer Schlenvogt on Idle Hours Resort
fields.author: Jennifer Schlenvogt
fields.body:
Quiet, family-friendly resort that feels like home, away from home. Owners are so friendly, cabins are clean and well-stocked, boats are well maintained. Our favorite things to do are kayaking around the peaceful Lake, the abundance of fish to catch and swimming with friends we've met that come the same week every year. Nearby mini golf, go-karts, great restaurants, and our new favorite, ATV trails.  Bring your own, and drive 100+ miles on beautifully groomed trails right out the back door of your cabin.
We've been coming for 5 years now, and it's the high point of our family's summer. Every year is better than the last.
This is a great vacation to disconnect from life, and reconnect as a family!!

fields.title: John L on The Salon Outback
fields.author: John L
fields.body:
I just wanted to give a big shoutout to Grace for doing such a beautiful job on my mom’s nails! She was incredibly kind, professional, and attentive to every detail. My mom couldn’t stop smiling afterward – she felt so pampered and special. Grace took the time to make sure everything was perfect, and the end result was absolutely stunning. Highly recommend her for anyone looking for quality nail care with a personal touch!

And….

I can’t say enough great things about Ashley! She recently did my mom’s hair, and the results were nothing short of amazing. From the moment we walked in, Ashley made my mom feel so comfortable and cared for. Her warm personality, attention to detail, and genuine passion for what she does truly set her apart.

She took the time to listen to exactly what my mom wanted and offered expert suggestions that made the final look even better than we imagined. My mom left feeling beautiful, confident, and pampered—and she hasn’t stopped getting compliments since!

Ashley is a true professional and an incredibly talented stylist. We’re beyond grateful for her skill and kindness, and we’ll definitely be returning. Highly recommend to anyone looking for top-notch hair care and a stylist who really goes above and beyond!

fields.title: Stephanie Bilbrey on Days Inn by Wyndham Eagle River
fields.author: Stephanie Bilbrey
fields.body:
The best part was the location- right on the trails. We were able to hop on sleds and go.

Absolutely listen and take into consideration the feedback on staff. The morning to afternoon staff - awesome, kind, attentive and informative. The evening/overnight staff acted like he hated his job. When we got there check in was less than informative. He gave us a general direction and seemed like he was half asleep.
The pool is NOT open 24/7 and closes at 10. The pool was an ice bath at best. I don't think the heater is working well if at all. The hot tub was nice.

Breakfast 10/10 delicious. Well stocked and fresh. Standard breakfast options and was enough to feel like you could have what you want without getting decision fatigue.

All in all a solid 2.5-3. it's a hotel, the shower is hot, the bed is comfy and there was no signs of bugs.

fields.author: Eric Heuser
fields.body: We had a good experience the last time we were there, but not this time! Our room this time didnt have a nice bathroom. We were in 137. Didnt like the shower or the shower floor. No kleenex at all! There was a plastic chair in the shower with a cheap shower curtain. When you would take a shower, water would go on floor almost forming a small puddle trailing under the door. No shelf or shower holder to place shampoo , soap or body wash and lighting in bathroom was bad. Dim lighting with no lighting above mirror. Also , there was virtually no place to park, because there were snowmobile trailers and trucks in front and back taking up alot of space. We wanted to go in the hot tub, but were unable to both nights, because it was full of 4-5 people. It was great the last time we were here, but not this time.

fields.title: Patricia O on Antler Crossings
fields.author: Patricia O
fields.body: My family has been going here for summer vacation for the past 5 years. It is a wonderful cabin with 4 bedrooms and 2 bathrooms. The kitchen is perfect for a cabin. My children love the pizza oven. The cabin is beautifully decorated. The beds are comfortable. Having a washer and dryer in the cabin is so useful. The cabin has WiFi which works as well as any WiFi in the Northwoods.  Outside the cabin is a picnic table, grill, and fire pit just for this cabin. Shared with the other cabins is a playset, shuffle board and horseshoes. For this cabin only is a canoe, paddle boat and row boat. The beach is great and it is shallow water quite aways out so it is wonderful for kids to play. The owners have been very responsive if there's anything needed. We look forward to going back each year.

fields.author: Chris W
fields.body: Our weeks stay was overshadowed and shortened by a fly infestation apparently caused by some dead animals that the owner killed under the cabin.  The shower didn't work, and the owner is unwilling to admit that there was a problem, instead accusing my family of causing issues for them...  The internet connection hardly ever worked, and the kitchen utensils and equipment leaves a lot to be desired.  I have stayed here before and wasn't really impressed but it wasn't terrible either, until now.  The owner was unwilling to take care of the problems while we were there, and didn't really even respond after we left, I had to search them out to try to come to a resolution, only to be accused of causing issues for them, apparently they had to clean the place...

fields.title: Deborah Glime on The Estrold Resort
fields.author: Deborah Glime
fields.body:
We started a new family tradition last summer of coming to The Estrold which we hope to continue for years to come. First, there are activities for kids to do, tennis, basketball, game room, volleyball, gnome hunting, and fishing derby to name a few.
Second, the cottage we stayed it was so cozy and cute and perfect for our family. It was close to the water and beach but didn't feel too busy with other guests near by.
Third, Amanda and Travis were attentive to our needs. We had an issue with a pontoon that was fixed and we were out on the water in no time! It is also nice that the garbage is picked up right at your cottage daily!
We liked the feel of the old-time resort, something that our family grew up going to and that's the feeling we got there. The kids really enjoyed it too and can't wait to go back and have fun on the paddle boards, kayaks, and paddle boat!
Unwind and unplug at The Estrold and enjoy all that St. Germain and the surrounding area has to offer!

Third, Amanda and Travis were attentive to our needs. We had an issue with a pontoon that was fixed and we were out on the water in no time! It is also nice that the garbage is picked up right at your cottage daily!
We liked the feel of the old-time resort, something that our family grew up going to and that's the feeling we got there. The kids really enjoyed it too and can't wait to go back and have fun on the paddle boards, kayaks, and paddle boat!
Unwind and unplug at The Estrold and enjoy all that St. Germain and the surrounding area has to offer!

fields.title: Mark Beemsterboer on The Estrold Resort
fields.author: Mark Beemsterboer
fields.body:
Update 10/7/2021:
The new owners are very friendly, and they obviously care about the resort; they've made many improvements since owning the business. The resort is in good hands.

Review from 2014:
I visited St. Germain, after a long absence, with my brother and sister in October. We couldn't have been more pleased with our cottage, the Bayview, nor with the resort at large. The grounds are beautiful, the cabins spacious and well appointed, and the Burgers made for wonderful hosts (warm and kind). Our cabin's size and appearance were total surprises; it was clean, beautiful, and perfectly ready for our visit.

The Estrold Resort is exactly what we hoped it would be: a resort experience from yesteryear without feeling rundown or sad. Far from it.

We visited not only for the fall colors, but because of my dad, who had died exactly 20 years ago to the day of our arrival at the resort. We visited hoping to make St. Germain into a happy place for us again, as it was when were kids during those magical summers. I was so hesitant to visit St. Germain that I stayed away for 24 years. That still astonishes me.

I can't express how important and wonderful this trip was for my sister, my brother, and for myself. Our time at the Estrold Resort was simply perfect.

fields.title: Lisa O. on Jackson's Lakeside Cottages
fields.author: Lisa O.
fields.body:
Our family had a wonderful vacation at Jackson's, and we hope to return next year.

CABINS:
There are three generations of cabins, and my family rented one from each. We stayed in Hammond (the newest), Memories, and Idle Hour (the oldest).

The kitchens are fully stocked with a skillet, sauce pans, sheet pans, can opener, eating and serving utensils, plates, bowls, mugs, drinking glasses,  ice trays, cutting boards, and knives. For appliances, there is a propane gas stove, full-sized refrigerator, toaster, and coffee pot with filters. The Hammond has a dishwasher. We brought our own bed linens, towels, toilet paper, hand soap, and dish soap.

Each cabin has a lovely screened-in porch. They also come with a charcoal Weber grill and picnic tables.

Overall, I am very pleased with the cabins. My only suggestion for improvement would be for the resort to increase the lighting in Idle Hour. The lighting is very dark inside, and some extra lamps would help to brighten the rooms. The other cabins did not have an issue with lighting.

PROPERTY:
The resort has two fire pits - one by the lake and one at the back of the cabins. The firewood is provided. The fire pit near the lake is my favorite place on the property! The fire pit is surrounded by very comfortable seating, and there are outlets nearby if needed.

The resort also has a playground and a large field where deer gather at dusk every evening.

Boats are available for rent, and the Jacksons also provide life vests and pool noodles (free of charge!) in the shed near the lake.

Overall, the property is lovely. The many trees are great for hanging camping hammocks. The path down to the lake is too steep for my grandma to walk, but luckily, there is a dirt road that leds directly to the fire pit and piers.

CELL RECEPTION AND WIFI:
We had some trouble getting cell phone reception. The nearest cell tower is AT&T. The Jackson’s Lodge has WiFi which reaches (weakly) to the Hammond.

PETS:
The resort is dog-friendly. There are some friendly dogs that live on the resort that happily roam around during the day.

OTHER:
We love that the resort is near Eagle River, where we enjoy gift shopping. There is also a nearby Sentry Foods grocery store in St. Germain.

CONCLUSION:
We loved our vacation at Jackson’s and would highly recommend it as a family-friendly vacation spot.

This cabin has plastic chairs that are covered in dirt. The picnic table is uneven and you can’t sit on it. There are ant hills everywhere in the grass. No air conditioning. Remember relaxing on the lake is more then what it seems so take yourself out and appreciate the land and it's glorious presence.

fields.author: Patricia Kay
fields.body:
I booked 3 rooms for our family members. The hotel has been upgraded so it was modern and tasteful. Our rooms were clean and we had everything we needed. My only complaint was the fact that there are no elevators so it was a little inconvenient for those of us in our 70's-I was not aware of this upon booking the rooms. This is why I didn't give the room 5 stars.
There were 10 of us and we appreciated the fact that were able to eat our take out dinner in the breakfast area. The pool and hot tub were adequate and enjoyed by our grandchildren. The continental breakfast was basic but everyone found something for a quick bite before we were on our way.

fields.body: The people are very friendly at the location. But I have been trying for 4 months to close out my grandmothers account after she passed away. I have filed all the necessary paperwork with the courthouse and state, yet they still will not close or pay out the account. I have tried calling customer service, management, and life events for US Bank, and they say to go in branch because they cannot assist but in branch cannot assist either. I have had to take off several days of work to travel to the nearest location and am always told I need one more thing yet then the next time it’s one more thing again. It should not be this difficult when state of Wisconsin and the government has all their regulations posted online and I have been through this process several times and it’s never been this difficult at other financials. Not to mention now the account has a $0 balance after I was told there was hundreds of dollars in the account last month. Would not recommend US Bank EVER!

fields.title: Jean K on Pirates Hideaway
fields.author: Jean K
fields.body:
This place is a jewel adventure for a day! We  bought our two tickets online earlier in the week, as I could see they fill up. We got there early enough to go to the tiki bar prior to our 12:30 ride. The bartender told us he could give us a bucket with ice to pack a few drinks for the ride. Everyone here is very friendly. We got on board to a friendly pirate greeting. We sat on top deck. Both pirates did a great job socializing and informing all of the people on the boat about the lakes, history, history of when they bought  it, etc. Then the ride began. What an amazing 90 minute tour. Small kids got to come steer the boat which I could tell by the look on their faces that they were thrilled to death. They provide water and lemonade when you ride and also come around with snacks mid way during the ride. This chain of lakes is beautiful with some amazing real estate to view. We saw two eagles nests and one eagle perked high in the tree watching our boat. Amazing. I didn't know what to expect when I booked this adventure but I can tell you it surpassed expectations. To go on a beautiful boat ride on a gorgeous hot Wisconsin day is a treat. Book this for sure. There is also food and pizza back at the tiki bar.
They have a patio upstairs and live music on certain days of the week; check on their website for details.  Next time we would like to be there in the evening also to enjoy a different atmosphere and their beautiful patio and lights.  We hope to make this a tradition and have other family members go next time too. I am adding lots of photos. Pirate Captain Steve is great and his son Steve both do a great job as fun pirates and truly seem to love what they do. Thank you to them!

fields.title: Nikki Schroeder on Lazy Bear Lodge on snow /UTV trails and lake
fields.author: Nikki Schroeder
fields.body:
‼️A Northwoods Gem‼️
​If you're looking for a getaway in the Wisconsin Northwoods without sacrificing modern comfort, the Lazy Bear Lodge is the destination for you. It’s a 2-bedroom, 2-bathroom condo on Little St. Germain Lake with access to miles of beautiful trails.

*​The open-concept living area features large windows with stunning views of the lake and a beautiful stone fireplace.
*​Having two full bathrooms is a game-changer for those of us traveling with a family or another couple.
*​The kitchen is fully equipped with everything you need to cook a hearty breakfast before hitting the trails.
*​You are literally on the trail. No need to trailer your sleds to a trailhead. You can suit up in the warmth of the condo and be on the trail system in seconds.There is plenty of storage space for your gear.
*The proximity to local pitstops and gas stations via trail is perfect.
*The hosts' communication from booking to checkout was phenomenal. The day before we arrived we received a message with everything we needed to know.

​There’s nothing quite like waking up to snow covered trees, an ice covered lake, hopping on the sleds for a 100+ mile loop, and coming back to a warm condo with a beautiful lake view. It makes for a stress-free Northwoods vacation. ​This condo captures the Up North magic. It’s CLEAN, cozy, and located in the heart of St. Germain’s best recreation. We enjoyed our stay! Don't think twice about booking with Scott & Missy!

fields.title: Dan S on Eagle Waters Resort & Supper Club
fields.author: Dan S
fields.body: Absolutely fabulous accommodations, clean, and quiet rooms with a great view! Theres also actual cabins for rent too if you have a group or family. The restaurant is gorgeous! Its like stepping back in time. I spent half an hour just looking at the decorations lol. Fish fry was amazing, hand cut ribeye was fabulous, and the pizza is better than Chicago, they make the sauce homemade in house. The prime is fantastic as well, a homemade rub is flavored perfectly, and oh boy its juicy. If its busy... expect a wait, but its worth it! Have a old fashioned or 2 at the bar and you won't even notice lol. The bartenders Ive met, Olivia and Bob, are super friendly. There are some gluten free options too! Gluten free pizza is my friend's favorite. Fantastic experience, Ive stayed and ate here numerous times and haven't been disappointed yet. Although summer when they are packed, its a wait... but you get that anywhere in summer!

fields.title: Michelle on Eagle Waters Resort & Supper Club
fields.author: Michelle
fields.body:
We had our daughters wedding at Eagle Waters Resort on October 18, 2025, and it truly was the most wonderful and magical day! Laura and her staff, (extra thanks to Brianna, Abby, Jackie) were beyond amazing to work with! From the moment we booked with them....they were professional, organized, and genuinely excited to help us bring 'our vision' to life!

We had the rehearsal dinner there Friday night, ceremony on site by the water Saturday and the reception after! Almost all of our out-of-town guest stayed right on site in the lodge or stayed in the many cabins on the grounds! So convenient! They took care of our every need from lodging help, ceremony and reception set up (and clean up after) along with providing the most amazing food!

Eagle Waters Resort will make your wedding or any event wonderful and memorable! So do not hesitate to book with them!

Thank you Eagke Waters for everything you did to make our daughter's wedding day one we will never forget! ~ The Rubo Family

fields.body: Don't waste your time. We made dinner reservations days prior and when we showed up, we had to stand in line for 20 mins just for service from the hostess. Then we were told it'd be over an hour wait for our table. When asked why such a wait with reservations, we were told it was because they only have 2 cooks. Sorry not sorry, but that is not my problem. Thats why i made reservations. We overhead several conversations from other guests stating they've been waiting nearly two hours for their tables. Horrible management and an embarrassingly dysfunctional system.  As parents who rarely get a night out for ourselves, this reserved time for us was more than just a dinner. This place used to be such a wonderful dining experience but we are truly disappointed and will not be going back. Truly a shame.

fields.author: Crystal Hladilek
fields.body: My family has stayed with Stiemke's for decades.  If that isn't the strongest testimony to the graciousness we have always experienced with them, I don't know what else to say.  We have been always met with a warm welcome, a comfortable and clean resort with a private beach for the kids, and friendly hosts.  There truly aren't enough positive words to describe my great memories and hope to continue them for decades to come!  Objectively speaking, it's centrally located in the northwoods, nice and close to St. Germain, twenty minutes from Eagle River and Minoqua, so fantastic location.  There are a variety of cabin sizes to meet your family's needs, whether you're just the pair of empty nesters or a family with many children.

fields.title: Jonny Diaco on Boat House Grill & Creamery
fields.author: Jonny Diaco
fields.body:
Great spot!  Props to the owner, who was flying solo due to a no-show and still managed to keep up and provide great service!
I got the burger, which was delicious, and an order of garlic cheese curds.  The curds were fire, but beware, they are GARLICKY!  I loved it.  Between the intensity of the garlic and the richness of the curds themselves, it's highly unlikely you'll be able to finish them yourself so share with a friend!  Maybe get the regular cheese curds if you're on a date... Or if you plan to be on one within the next week or so.  😆
Really great spot, will be back soon to get some ice cream!

fields.body: Lovely trail! Very well maintained, with great signage and plenty of options to adjust trail length. We hiked the “Green Trail” around Shannon Lake, which is 2.3 miles (took us right around 1 hour), and starts at the south parking area off Found Lake Road. Parking is also available off Hwy G to the north, and the full outer loop (Red Trail) is 7 miles.

fields.title: Jilly Dockerty on Wild Eagle Lodge
fields.author: Jilly Dockerty
fields.body: Wow!  What a great place to stay!  Our family has 2 kids with special needs, one with Down syndrome and mobility challenges.  Traveling is usually not relaxing, to say the least!  The Wild Eagle Lodge was one of the best experiences I have had in years with my special family!  The condo unit was beautiful, clean, and even accessible for our guy (including the shower in the main bathroom)!  Wifi was strong and the kitchen was well equipped.  It was spotless!  The trails were paved and beautiful.  We were able to use his wheelchair to get around and even get down to the piers!  The pool area was wonderful! And the staff was so courteous and helpful!  They even emailed me about an earring they found after we left and mailed it back to me. We will be back for sure!

we feel so violated. Stay somewhere else is my recommendation

fields.title: Lizzie Smiley on Just Yarnin'
fields.author: Lizzie Smiley
fields.body:
This is one of the best yarn shops I’ve ever been to. I’m a knitter and crocheter— and going in this place is always just like a hug.

Nancy Anne and Kim are so welcoming and knowledgeable and helpful. I absolutely adore them and can’t wait to see them each summer.

There’s a large selection of luscious yarn, all the supplies you could need, the most giftable project bags and notions, and SO many completed projects to inspire your next one!

Whether you’ve never knit before (they can suggest some starter supplies and the perfect beginner pattern) or you’re practically a pro— this place will have yarn and inspiration for you.

Local ladies come in each day to sit around the table, work on their projects, admire each other’s work, and offer help when someone is stuck. It’s everything we knitters want it to be.

I really hope to see you there and take a peek at what’s on your needles. 🩵

fields.author: Colleen Storiz
fields.body: My all-time favorite yarn shop even though I live over 5 hours away! Excellent selection of quality yarns, friendly and knowledgeable staff, beautiful samples, and so much more. Located in the heart of St.Germain, Wisconsin, it's my favorite place to go when visiting my family in the Northwoods. Nancy Anne and Kim have been so welcoming since the very first day I walked through their door asking for knitting lessons. Here we are, nearly 4, maybe 5 years later and I can't think of a better place to sit and knit, buy my yarn from, share knitting stories and life in general. I have a LYS near my home but I'd rather purchase my yarn here as they're always so kind and welcoming! 💖

fields.title: Vinny Ritchie on Trailside Log Cabins
fields.author: Vinny Ritchie
fields.body: -16 below temps & you could see the parking lot through the side of the door. Fireplace didn’t work. The ice fishing shack we rented for 2 days was frozen to boat ramp so couldn’t use it after buying bait & everything else. They wanted to still Chg us for 1 day & then later that day charged us 150.00 delivery fee. We never used the shack.The heater inside it hardly worked.One of rental sleds had a bad shock. Tried calling number for any snowmobile issues & no one answered or returned our calls. They said they never got any calls. We have proof on our phone we called 4 times. We went back to rental shop & no one was around, We swapped sleds with another sled that had keys left in it. & no one was there, place was closed. Another sled the handlebar warmers didn’t work. At below 0 temps it would have been nice.Came back again & swapped that sled out.Tried getting insurance for the sleds that they offered & Don said, too late. Should have done that when we signed up for sleds a month ago. I can go on & on but don’t have the time. This was a total disaster. Our vacation was ruined & we dearly paid for it.

fields.title: Parascovia Tulegenov on B Family at the Lake
fields.author: Parascovia Tulegenov
fields.body:
A peaceful forest escape. We had a wonderful stay at this beautiful forest cabin. This place feels like stepping into a quiet winter story. Surrounded by tall pine trees and a frozen lake, the atmosphere is incredibly peaceful and grounding. The wooden house blends beautifully with nature, and the large windows bring the forest right inside. Outside, there are wonderful spots to relax and enjoy nature. The dock over the frozen lake is stunning and feels like a place where you can sit with your thoughts and just breathe. The firepit area is perfect for spending time with family and friends, especially with kids playing around in the snow. There’s even a charming little sauna cabin that adds to the whole cozy forest experience.
Overall, it’s a place where time slows down. Perfect for reconnecting with nature, spending quality time with family, and enjoying simple beautiful moments. Peaceful, Cozy, Surrounded by nature, Perfect for a quiet getaway. Im happy that I spent 3 days of my life here, We would definitely love to come back.

The kids loved being outdoors just as much as all the indoor games. The owners were incredibly accommodating with great communication throughout. We already can’t wait to come back in the summer.

fields.title: Ana Ochisor on B Family at the Lake
fields.author: Ana Ochisor
fields.body:
This place is absolutely stunning, nestled in the heart of nature! The large lake provided the perfect setting for my first fishing experience, and I loved every moment of it. We were also lucky enough to spot deer nearby, which added to the charm of the location.
The house itself is spacious, cozy, and beautifully surrounded by tall trees and lush greenery. It’s impeccably clean, well maintained, and thoughtfully organized, making our stay incredibly comfortable.
We spent most of our time cooking outdoors, enjoying the fresh air, and dining while watching the sunset, truly magical. I would highly recommend this place to anyone looking for a peaceful, nature-filled getaway!

fields.title: Dorina Gadibadi on B Family at the Lake
fields.author: Dorina Gadibadi
fields.body:
A hidden gem in the heart of nature 🌲🐿️🦔🦌🐟🐛

I’ve had the pleasure of staying at this cabin across all four seasons, and every visit has been nothing short of magical. Nestled deep in the woods, it’s the perfect escape from the chaos of daily life. The cabin is immaculately clean, spacious, and beautifully designed with comfort in mind.

What truly sets this place apart are the amenities—a private sauna and hot tub right on the lake make for the most relaxing evenings, whether you’re watching the sunset in summer or snow falling in winter. The wildlife is incredible; I’ve even spotted a white deer 🥹

There’s never a dull moment—whether you enjoy kayaking, hiking, fishing, or just sitting by the fire with a book, there’s something for everyone. Each season brings its own charm: vibrant fall colors, peaceful snowy landscapes, blooming spring mornings, and warm, activity-filled summer days.

And I can’t say enough about the hosts—they’re incredibly kind, responsive, and always willing to help with anything you need. Communication was fast and easy every time, and they truly go above and beyond to make sure your stay is perfect.

If you’re looking for a peaceful, rejuvenating, and unforgettable getaway in nature, this cabin is it. I can’t recommend it enough! 🌿🛶🔥

The machines are sensitive to weight, so when you take an item from one bag to put in another the machine flags it.

fields.author: Trevur
fields.body: Me and my dads first time up in this area, we didn't really know our way around ice fishing wise, Eric helped us out a lot! We stopped in all three days of our trip, GREAT prices and a good conversation about recent conditions and fishing. We visited a few other stores in the area, no one was as kind and courteous as Eric. He really has the placed stocked with good lures, terminal tackle, rod and reel combos, lures & baits, and a lot of other fishing equipment, all this in a compact but not too small of a shop. If we come back up to this area again, we are DEFINITELY stopping here.

fields.body: Wild turkey is a great place to stop. The food is good, the layout is great, and the helmet wrack is great for snowmobilers. Mackenzie was our bartender and she sets a high standard, she was so helpful whether it came to drinks, food, or conversation, she was there and ready with a smile. If you are around the area on a Sunday, I highly recommend stopping in and experiencing it for yourself!

fields.body: We visited the Plaid Bear for the second time today as we live about 4 hours away and only make it up here once a year. When we showed up Friday the doors were closed, I looked them up on Facebook, to find they have limited hours this time of year as the owner was out of town. I thought I’d send a message and see if they had hours open when we would be there this weekend. Even though they did not have open hours this past weekend,  the owner happily and promptly message back and offered to have Barb come on in and open the place up just for us to shop! Barb was so incredibly sweet and helpful, took time out of her own Sunday to enjoy/talk and let us shop! We didn’t feel rushed at all and got all the adorable goodies this place has to offer! 10/5 stars! Such cute apparel and decor! Customer service from everyone was outstanding! Made us feel super special today! Can’t wait to go back next year ❤️

I was so excited walking into the store as some Christmas decor was being stocked and I wanted everything. I bought a sign for Little St. Germain Lake as a housewarming gift along with a plaid shirt for myself. My husband was ready to buy every ornament in there. The owner is a wonderful lady with great taste and I look forward to visiting the shop every time I visit my parents' cabin (which will be a lot)! Despite a rather condescending previous review on Google, the owner did wear her mask properly at all times.

fields.body: WOW, this me be the best step back in time in the north woods. We loved our service as well as the quality of our cocktails.  The staff are like an old friend you have not seen in a while, always nice and comfortable!

fields.title: Melissa Hintz on Buckshots Saloon & Eatery
fields.author: Melissa Hintz
fields.body: Stopped in on our way to Bond Falls. The place was packed, but we quickly got a table. I ordered a cranberry mule and it was delicious!  I got a second one, but I think that was actually the Cran-Apple, even better than the first!  Garlic cheese curds were great. I ordered a mushroom Swiss burger, which was good, but I would probably get something else next time. My boyfriend got the brisket Reuben and he said it was amazing. Our server was a true gem - it was so busy, but she was very attentive and made conversation with us. She had two long days ahead and then was heading on a well-deserved vacation!

fields.title: Daniel Eggen on Buckshots Saloon & Eatery
fields.author: Daniel Eggen
fields.body:
We had the pleasure of eating there one evening during our kiddos track meet between their events. The service was top notch and she was extremely friendly and warm. The food was also flavorful and delicious, so it’s a top spot for us now when in the area.

We also just used them for catering for 100 servings with a combination of smoked brisket and pulled pork, along with their pepper slaw. The team was great when we picked it up and went above what was quoted to ensure the event was successful. As for the party - Everyone loved the food and asked where it was from.

Would definitely recommend for dining or catering needs.

fields.title: Chris Lokrantz on Shotskis
fields.author: Chris Lokrantz
fields.body: Me and my brother take a trip every year going to different places. Stopped in to Shotskis to check it out. Definitely a 5 star bar. Place is clean and has a nice atmosphere. The people were really friendly. The bartender (owner) was amazing. One of the best we have came across in our travels. Would recommend this place to everyone that comes to or lives in Eagle River. Definitely will stop in again if our travels allow. Thanks for making our night a great one .
